summary: Deck page 7: '3,100 active installations across 240 organizations'. Deck page 9: '$18k MRR, +40% MoM for 3 months'. No external corroboration attached.
Repository: 412 stars, 61 forks, 9 external contributors. 1,400 commits over 11 months, 86% by the founder. Weekly release cadence for the last 20 weeks. CI config shows real test coverage.
Winner, infrastructure track, Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon May 2026 — 'Ferrite: content-addressed caching for agent CI'. Judged field of ~400 teams.
Public package registry: 340 downloads/week, 2,900 downloads total lifetime. Tension with deck's '3,100 active installations' — lifetime downloads are below the claimed active-install count.

Launch post, 2nd June: front page of a major aggregator for ~6 hours, 214 points, 89 comments. Founder answered 41 comments in-thread with technical depth; three commenters identified themselves as production users.

FICTIONAL FIXTURE — illustrative Founder Score for the invented Ferrite deal: 71 [58, 80], confidence medium, cold_start true (no prior fundraise, no prior company), rationale: shipping cadence (sig-002) and hackathon win (sig-003). Hand-written example of the founder.schema shape; NOT produced by the Memory layer (real Memory records live in memory/founders.json).
